description_long: This is a traditional stone water well set on a rectangular patch of earth, surrounded by scattered clay buckets. The well itself is cylindrical, constructed from rough, light-gray stone, and features a wooden gabled roof supported by two vertical posts. A metal crank handle is attached to one side, likely used to operate a rope or chain for drawing water. The roof is made of dark wooden planks arranged in a slatted pattern, providing partial cover over the well opening. The ground around the well is uneven and shows patches of green moss or algae, suggesting moisture. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden slatted roof, metal crank handle, and scattered clay buckets on a patch of earth.
description_view_0: Front view showing the well's stone body, wooden roof, metal crank handle, and three clay buckets on the ground.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slatted wooden roof, cylindrical stone well, and two clay buckets, one upright and one tipped over.
description_view_2: Rear view with the well's stone surface, wooden roof, crank handle, and three visible clay buckets, one lying on its side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden roof structure, stone well, and two clay buckets, one close to the well and one farther away.
attribute_well_cover_design: Wooden gabled roof made of slatted planks, supported by two posts; no lid or grating over the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A metal crank handle is present, but no visible pulley or suspended bucket; several clay buckets are placed around the well.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a low stone wall forming the well body; no additional fence, railing, or canopy is present.
